segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: No, it's entirely up to us.
segments.zh: 不，完全由我们决定。我建议就用查尔斯·狄更斯。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: I suggest just using Charles Dickens.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: That's a good idea.
segments.zh: 好主意。大多数人都听说过他，可能还读过他的一些小说，或者看过改编的电影，所以这是了解他那个时代生活的一个很好的切入点。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Most people have heard of him, and have probably read some of his novels, or seen films based on them, so that's a good lead-in to life in his time.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。他的小说展现了当时大多数人不得不生活的恶劣条件，不是吗？他想以此震撼人们，促使他们采取行动。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: And his novels show the awful conditions that most people had to live in, don't they: he wanted to shock people into doing something about it.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Did he do any campaigning, other than writing?
segments.zh: 除了写作，他有没有做过什么宣传活动？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Yes, he campaigned for education and other social reforms, and gave talks, but I'm inclined to ignore that and focus on the novels.
segments.zh: 有的，他倡导教育和其他社会改革，还发表过演讲，但我倾向于忽略这些，专注于小说。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Yes, I agree.
segments.zh: 是的，我同意。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: OK, so now shall we think about a topic linked to each novel?
segments.zh: 好的，那我们现在想想每部小说对应的主题？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Yes, I've printed out a list of Dickens's novels in the order they were published, in the hope you'd agree to focus on him!
segments.zh: 是的，我按出版顺序打印了一份狄更斯小说的清单，希望你会同意专注于他！

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: You're lucky I did agree!
segments.zh: 你运气好，我同意了！我们来看看。好吧，第一本是《匹克威克外传》，1836年出版。当时非常成功，对吧，而且立刻就被改编成了舞台剧。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Let's have a look.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: OK, the first was The Pickwick Papers, published in 1836.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: It was very successful when it came out, wasn't it, and was adapted for the theatre straight away.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: There's an interesting point, though, that there's a character who keeps falling asleep, and that medical condition was named after the book – Pickwickian Syndrome.
segments.zh: 不过有个有趣的点，书里有个角色老是睡着，后来那种医学状况就以这本书命名——匹克威克综合征。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Oh, so why don't we use that as the topic, and include some quotations from the novel?
segments.zh: 哦，那我们不如就用这个作为主题，再引用一些小说里的句子？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。下一本是《雾都孤儿》。小说里有很多关于贫困的内容。但也许可以选个不那么明显的角度……

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Next is Oliver Twist.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: There's a lot in the novel about poverty.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: But maybe something less obvious ...

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Well Oliver is taught how to steal, isn't he?
segments.zh: 嗯，奥利弗被教怎么偷东西，对吧？我们可以用这个来说明当时很少有孩子上学，特别是穷孩子，所以他们通过其他方式学习。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: We could use that to illustrate the fact that very few children went to school, particularly not poor children, so they learnt in other ways.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Good idea.
segments.zh: 好主意。下一个是什么？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: What's next?

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Maybe Nicholas Nickleby.
segments.zh: 也许是《尼古拉斯·尼克尔贝》。实际上他教书的学校很残酷，对吧？

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Actually he taught in a really cruel school, didn't he?

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。但尼古拉斯还加入了一个巡回演员剧团。我们可以做点关于当时的剧院和其他娱乐活动的内容。我们不想只讲坏的一面，对吧？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: But there's also the company of touring actors that Nicholas joins.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: We could do something on theatres and other amusements of the time.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: We don't want only the bad things, do we?

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: What about Martin Chuzzlewit?
segments.zh: 那《马丁·瞿述伟》呢？他去了美国，对吧？

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: He goes to the USA, doesn't he?

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Yes, and Dickens himself had been there a year before, and drew on his experience there in the novel.
segments.zh: 是的，狄更斯本人一年前去过那里，并在小说中借鉴了他在那里的经历。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: I wonder, though ...
segments.zh: 不过我在想……小说的主题是自私，所以我们可以做点关于社会正义的内容？不，太笼统了，还是坚持你的想法吧——我觉得会效果不错。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: The main theme is selfishness, so we could do something on social justice?

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: No, too general, let's keep to your idea – I think it would work well.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: He wrote Bleak House next – that's my favourite of his novels.
segments.zh: 他接下来写了《荒凉山庄》——那是我最喜欢他的小说。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Yes, mine too.
segments.zh: 是的，我也是。他对法律体系的讽刺相当有力。

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: His satire of the legal system is pretty powerful.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: That's true, but think about Esther, the heroine.
segments.zh: 没错，但想想女主角埃斯特。她小时候和一个她不知道是自己姨妈的人住在一起，那人对她很不好。后来她和监护人住在一起非常开心，监护人让她管理家务。最后她结婚了，监护人和她丈夫给了他们一栋房子，他们当然过得很幸福。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: As a child she lives with someone she doesn't know is her aunt, who treats her very badly.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Then she's very happy living with her guardian, and he puts her in charge of the household.

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: And at the end she gets married and her guardian gives her and her husband a house, where of course they're very happy.

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: Yes, I like that.
segments.zh: 是的，我喜欢这个。

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: What shall we take next?
segments.zh: 我们接下来该读什么？《小杜丽》？老杜丽先生已经在债务人监狱里待了好多年了……

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Little Dorrit?

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: Old Mr Dorrit has been in a debtors' prison for years ...

segments.speaker: GRAHAM
segments.en: So was Dickens's father, wasn't he?
segments.zh: 狄更斯的父亲也是这样，对吧？

segments.speaker: CATHY
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。

items.prompt: Which TWO groups of people is the display primarily intended for?
Which TWO groups of people is the display primarily intended for?
What are Cathy and Graham's TWO reasons for choosing the novelist Charles Dickens?

pairs.p: wanted to shock people into doing something about it
pairs.note: 题干问选择狄更斯的原因，原文用‘想要震撼人们采取行动’解释了他的动机，这是原因的同义表达。
题干中的‘小说家查尔斯·狄更斯’在原文中简化为‘他的小说’，指代相同。

paraphrase.explain: 通过‘reasons’对应‘wanted to shock’，以及‘novelist’对应‘his novels’定位到原文。

items.prompt: What are Cathy and Graham's TWO reasons for choosing the novelist Charles Dickens?

pairs.p: wanted to shock people into doing something about it
pairs.note: 题干问选择原因，原文用‘wanted to...’直接说明目的，是原因的同义表达。
题干提到作家狄更斯，原文用‘his novels’指代他的作品，属于同一主题。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接给出了选择狄更斯的原因：他想通过小说震惊人们并促使行动。题干中的‘reasons’对应原文的‘wanted to...’，因此答案就是这句话。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?

pairs.note: 题干问'topic'，原文用'interesting point'替换，表示讨论的主题。
题干说'illustrate'（例证），原文说'named after the book'（以书命名），说明小说被用来例证一种医学状况。

paraphrase.explain: 通过'interesting point'定位到讨论的主题，再根据'named after the book'找到小说被用来例证的内容，从而确定答案G。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?

pairs.note: 题干和原文都用了illustrate，直接对应。

pairs.p: the fact that very few children went to school
pairs.note: 题干问topic，原文具体说明了主题内容。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的illustrate和原文中的illustrate直接对应，以及原文中具体说明的主题内容，可以定位到答案。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?

pairs.p: something on theatres and other amusements
pairs.note: 题干问‘topic’，原文用‘something on...’表示话题内容。
题干‘illustrate with each novel’对应原文‘We could do something on’，表示选择话题来展示。

paraphrase.explain: 通过原文‘We could do something on theatres and other amusements’定位到话题是‘theatres and other amusements’，对应选项D。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?

pairs.note: 题干说'用小说说明主题'，原文说'借鉴经历'，意思相近。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'用哪部小说说明什么主题'，原文说狄更斯借鉴经历写小说，所以选C。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?

pairs.note: 题干中的'illustrate with each novel'（用每部小说举例）被原文中的'think about Esther, the heroine'（想想女主角Esther）替换，即通过具体人物来举例说明主题。

paraphrase.explain: 通过原文中'think about Esther, the heroine'这一具体例子，可以定位到他们讨论的小说内容，从而推断出他们选择的主题。

items.prompt: What topic do Cathy and Graham choose to illustrate with each novel?
